definitions: To inadvertently make a situation worse for oneself.
To cause oneself harm or disadvantage through one's own actions or words.
examples: He really shot himself in the foot by insulting the boss.
Don't shoot yourself in the foot by procrastinating on this project.
They shot themselves in the foot with that poorly planned marketing campaign.
lemma: to shoot oneself in the foot
synonyms: to cut off one's nose to spite one's face
to paint oneself into a corner
source: to shoot oneself in the foot
